data.title: Two Women Convicted in $9.4 Million Amazon Fraud Scheme
data.description: Two women have been found guilty of orchestrating a fraudulent scheme that defrauded Amazon of nearly $9.4 million. According to NS3.AI, the scheme involved submitting fake vendor invoices to the company. The ill-gotten gains were reportedly used to purchase luxury vehicles and a $1 million home. One of the defendants received her sentence in 2023, while the other is scheduled to be sentenced on June 16, 2026.

data.title: Drones Repelled Near Oil Refineries in Russia's Bashkortostan Region
data.description: Authorities in Russia's Bashkortostan region reported that drones were repelled overnight near major oil refineries. Bloomberg posted on X that these refineries had been targeted multiple times last year. The incident highlights ongoing security concerns in the area, which is crucial for Russia's oil production. No further details were provided about the drones or the measures taken to counter them.

data.title: Iran Issues Highest Denomination Banknote Amid Inflation Concerns
data.description: Tehran has introduced a new banknote with a denomination of 10 million rials, equivalent to approximately $7, marking the highest denomination in the country's history. According to Jin10, the Iranian authorities aim to curb soaring inflation and address cash demand during the ongoing conflict involving the U.S. and Israel. This week, long queues have formed at ATMs across Iran due to fears of potential electronic system failures, prompting banks to distribute the new banknotes.

data.title: Robinhood CEO: Urges Congress to Allow Stablecoin Yields
data.description: Robinhood CEO Vlad Tenev urged the U.S. Congress to allow stablecoin yields, stating that consumers should receive returns on their stablecoin holdings in the same way they would on a savings account. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Iranian Media Reports: Israel Strikes Natanz Uranium Enrichment Facility
data.description: Israel has reportedly launched another attack on Iran's Natanz uranium enrichment facility, according to Iranian media. The facility has not reported any leakage of radioactive materials following the incident. The attack marks a continuation of tensions between the two nations, with previous incidents involving similar targets. According to Jin10, the Natanz facility is a key site in Iran's nuclear program, which has been a point of contention in international relations. The Iranian government has yet to release an official statement regarding the attack or its implications for ongoing diplomatic efforts.

data.title: Global Economy Faces Challenges Amid Varied Economic Indicators
data.description:
This week, the global economy experienced a mix of challenges and developments, as highlighted by various economic indicators. Bloomberg posted on X, noting that inflation rates in several major economies showed signs of easing, yet concerns about potential recessions persist.

In the United States, recent data indicated a slowdown in consumer spending, raising questions about the strength of the economic recovery. Meanwhile, the European Central Bank maintained its interest rates, signaling caution amid ongoing economic uncertainties in the Eurozone.

China's economic growth continues to be a focal point, with recent figures showing a slight improvement in manufacturing output. However, analysts remain cautious about the sustainability of this growth given the ongoing trade tensions and domestic challenges.

In the commodities market, oil prices fluctuated due to geopolitical tensions and supply chain disruptions, impacting global trade dynamics. Additionally, the cryptocurrency market saw volatility, with Bitcoin experiencing significant price swings throughout the week.

Overall, the global economic landscape remains complex, with various factors influencing market stability and growth prospects. Policymakers and investors are closely monitoring these developments to navigate the uncertain economic environment.

data.title: Iran's Revolutionary Guards: No Immediate Risk to Residents Near Natanz Uranium Enrichment Facility After Attack
data.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guards have reported that there is no immediate risk to residents living near the Natanz uranium enrichment facility following a recent attack. According to Jin10, the incident has raised concerns about the safety and security of the area, but officials have assured the public that measures are in place to protect local communities. The attack on the facility, which plays a crucial role in Iran's nuclear program, has drawn international attention and heightened tensions in the region. Authorities are currently investigating the circumstances surrounding the attack and are working to ensure the continued safety of the facility and its surroundings.

data.title: Iranian armed forces claim to be conducting major operations in the Strait of Hormuz.
data.description: A spokesperson for the central headquarters of the Iranian Armed Forces in Hatem-Anbia stated on the 21st local time that Iran is "taking significant action in the Strait of Hormuz." The spokesperson emphasized, "If Iran attacks its infrastructure, Iran will attack its more important and critical infrastructure." (CCTV News)

data.title: This week, US Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a net inflow of $93.1 million.
data.description: According to Farside monitoring data, US Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a net inflow of $93.1 million this week.

data.title: Austria Faces €2 Billion Budget Savings Amid Economic Concerns
data.description: Austria is required to identify up to €2 billion in budget savings as part of its fiscal consolidation efforts, according to the country's finance minister. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the growing risks to Austria's economic outlook due to the ongoing conflict in Iran. The finance minister emphasized the importance of these savings to stabilize the national budget and mitigate potential economic challenges. The situation underscores the broader impact of geopolitical tensions on European economies, necessitating careful financial planning and adjustments.

data.title: Worldcoin to Host World ID Launch Event in Los Angeles
data.description: Worldcoin is set to hold its World ID launch event, named Lift Off, in Los Angeles on April 17. According to NS3.AI, Sam Altman, the CEO of OpenAI, is anticipated to be present at the event.

data.title: Qatar Investment Authority Plans Stake Acquisition in Golden Goose
data.description: Qatar Investment Authority is set to acquire approximately 10% of Italian sneaker manufacturer Golden Goose, according to a report by Corriere della Sera. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the strategic move by the sovereign wealth fund to expand its investment portfolio in the fashion industry. This acquisition aligns with Qatar's ongoing efforts to diversify its assets globally. Golden Goose, known for its high-end sneakers, has been gaining popularity in international markets, making it an attractive investment opportunity. The deal underscores Qatar's interest in tapping into the luxury goods sector, which continues to show resilience amid economic fluctuations.

data.title: Iranian Foreign Minister says he seeks a complete end to the war, not just a temporary ceasefire.
data.description: According to a Kyodo News report on the 21st, Iranian Foreign Minister Araqchi stated that Iran hopes for a complete, comprehensive, and lasting end to the war, not just a temporary ceasefire. In a telephone interview with a Kyodo News reporter on the 20th, Araqchi said that the attacks on Iran by the United States and Israel were illegal and unjustified acts of aggression, and he hoped the world would unite to oppose them. Several countries have begun to push for a ceasefire, but Iran is only considering a solution that ends the war decisively. Iran does not accept a temporary ceasefire but demands a complete end to the war, including ensuring that Iran will not be attacked again and compensation for the losses suffered. Araqchi also said he does not believe the United States is ready for negotiations. The report stated that Araqchi also indicated his intention to allow Japanese vessels to pass through the Strait of Hormuz, a vital energy transport route, after consultation with Japan. (Jinshi)

data.title: NATO Urged to Maintain Strong Deterrence Amid Middle East Focus
data.description: NATO must ensure it does not reduce its presence along its flanks to maintain a credible deterrence against Russian aggression, according to Norway's defense minister. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the importance of NATO's strategic positioning at a time when the U.S. is concentrating on the conflict in the Middle East. The minister emphasized that any retrenchment could weaken the alliance's ability to respond effectively to potential threats from Russia.

data.title: Ju.com's Meme-based IPO project, Punch, continues to hit new highs, with a more than 10-fold increase in just 3 days.
data.description: Ju.com's "Meme Phase 10 Initial Coin Offering Plan" Phase 2 project, PUNCH, has seen its price surge since launch, reaching a high of 4,700 USDT. This represents a more than 10-fold increase in the past 3 days, a 4.7 billion-fold increase from the initial offering price of 0.000001 USDT. For more details, please refer to Ju.com's official announcements and event page.

data.title: Thailand's Industrial GDP Faces Potential Decline Amid Middle East Tensions
data.description:
Thailand's Office of Industrial Economics (OIE) has issued a warning regarding the potential impact of rising crude oil prices on the country's industrial GDP. According to Jin10, if tensions in the Middle East lead to an increase in oil prices to $100-105 per barrel, Thailand's industrial GDP could shrink by 100-120 billion baht, accounting for approximately 0.15% of the sector's GDP.

The industries most likely to be affected are those with high energy consumption, including cement and concrete, glass and flat glass, ceramics and tiles, natural gas and petroleum, textiles and apparel, and pulp and paper. In contrast, Thailand's major high-value industries, such as electronics, gems and jewelry, hard disk drives, electrical appliances, and automotive manufacturing, have lower energy intensity and are expected to experience a more limited overall impact.

data.title: Shiba Inu ETF Speculation Linked to T. Rowe's Revised Filing
data.description: T. Rowe's recent revision of its ETF filing, associated with nearly $1.8 trillion in assets under management, is sparking bullish speculation around Shiba Inu. According to NS3.AI, the potential approval of a SHIB ETF could provide a liquidity boost for the cryptocurrency. However, the commentary also highlights that sustained demand would be crucial following any approval to maintain momentum.

data.title: Fire at South Korean Factory Claims 11 Lives, Search for Missing Continues
data.description:
A devastating fire at an auto parts manufacturing plant in Daejeon, central South Korea, has resulted in at least 11 fatalities and 59 injuries. According to RTHK, rescue teams discovered another body at noon today, while three individuals remain missing. The blaze erupted around 1 p.m. yesterday, with 170 workers present in the facility. Concerns over the building's structural integrity initially prevented firefighters from entering to conduct searches. The fire was eventually extinguished after approximately 10 and a half hours.

Rescue personnel are currently using heavy machinery to clear debris and continue the search for those still unaccounted for. Authorities have announced the formation of a special task force to handle the aftermath of the fire. A joint investigation with relevant departments will be conducted to determine the cause of the incident.

data.title: Iranian Media Reports Multiple Explosions in Eastern Tehran
data.description: At least three explosions have occurred in the eastern part of Tehran, Iran's capital. According to Jin10, the incidents were reported by Al Jazeera, highlighting the ongoing tensions in the region. Details about the cause and impact of the explosions remain unclear, and authorities have yet to release an official statement. The situation is developing, and further updates are expected as more information becomes available.
